steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between "Puerto Rico's" and "showcase everything from…" The word before the blank is the possessive "Puerto Rico's," which needs a noun to complete the subject. The verb "showcase" follows immediately after. The rule: nothing may separate a subject from its verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — museums: no punctuation between subject ("Puerto Rico's museums") and verb ("showcase") — grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice B — museums:: a colon cannot separate a subject from its verb. ✗
  • Choice C — museums,: a comma cannot separate a subject from its verb. ✗
  • Choice D — museums—: an opening em-dash cannot separate a subject from its verb without a closing em-dash to finish the interruption. ✗
That is the correct answer.
